title: 149. Max Points on a Line
question:
Given n points on a 2D plane, find the maximum number of points that lie on the same straight line.

Example 1:

Input: [[1,1],[2,2],[3,3]]
Output: 3
Explanation:
^
|
|        o
|     o
|  o  
+------------->
0  1  2  3  4

Example 2:

Input: [[1,1],[3,2],[5,3],[4,1],[2,3],[1,4]]
Output: 4
Explanation:
^
|
|  o
|     o        o
|        o
|  o        o
+------------------->
0  1  2  3  4  5  6

NOTE: input types have been changed on April 15, 2019. Please reset to default code definition to get new method signature.
code: # Definition for a point.\u000Aclass Point(object):\u000A    def __init__(self, a\u003D0, b\u003D0):\u000A        self.x \u003D a\u000A        self.y \u003D b\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def maxPoints(self, points):\u000A        import numpy as np\u000A        max_count \u003D 0\u000A        for i, point1 in enumerate(points):\u000A            x1, y1 \u003D point1.x, point1.y\u000A            slope_cnt \u003D {}\u000A            same \u003D 0\u000A            for j, point2 in enumerate(points):\u000A                if i !\u003D j:\u000A                    x2, y2 \u003D point2.x, point2.y\u000A                    if (x1, y1) !\u003D (x2, y2):\u000A                        slope \u003D np.longdouble(y2 \u002D y1) / (x2 \u002D x1) if (x2 !\u003D x1) else \u0027inf\u0027\u000A                        slope_cnt[slope] \u003D slope_cnt.get(slope, 1) + 1\u000A                    else:\u000A                        same +\u003D 1\u000A            max_count \u003D max(max(slope_cnt.values() or [1]) + same, max_count)\u000A        return max_count or int(bool(points))\u000A
